goal.summary: Implement the bounded P1 provider work-order and receipt binding slice.

commands_run.command: uv run ruff check --select F,I src/tau_coding/generic_provider_adapter.py tests/test_generic_provider_adapter.py

commands_run.result: No whitespace errors reported for tracked diffs

what_changed: Generic provider node receipts now include a tau.provider_dag_node_binding.v1 subreceipt.
Canonical tau.provider_dag_work_order.v1 inputs are checked for node id, DAG id, goal hash, attempt, Herdr workspace, pane, terminal, and visible log hash binding.
Canonical binding failures block the generic provider node receipt even when the provider DAG status claims PASS.
Legacy non-canonical work orders remain hash-carried as LEGACY_UNBOUND for backward compatibility.
proves: The generic provider adapter deterministically accepts a canonical work order when matching Herdr record and visible log evidence are present.
The adapter blocks a canonical work order with a mismatched node_id.
The adapter blocks a canonical work order when the matching Herdr provider record lacks a visible_log_path.
The adjacent generic DAG and provider adapter tests pass locally.
does_not_prove: A real Herdr workspace or pane was created during this proof.
Full P1 Herdr provider work-order schema enforcement across every runtime entrypoint.
Provider branch locks, route mutation, Memory sync, GitHub mutation, or browser UI proof.

next_recommended_route.reason: Extend canonical provider work-order requirements from the adapter boundary into the provider DAG runtime entrypoints and real-world sanity fixtures.
